title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, from Marston Mills, was arrested for allegedly stabbing friend Brian Gordon at a Miami Beach party.
The stabbing incident happened on Ocean Drive during a bachelor party after a dispute over attention from women at local bars.
Both men were injured and taken to the hospital; the knife reportedly matched injuries sustained by both Dickerson and Gordon.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four games this season.
Ilay Feingold scored early for Revolution, but St. Louis responded with three unanswered goals, including a set-piece finish.
Forward Leo Campana exited with a new injury, unrelated to his previous one, in his first game back from a two-and-a-half-week absence.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts and New Hampshire authorities warn against fake court notices sent by scammers with numerous red flags.
The scam includes incorrect details like "State of Massachuseetts" and asks recipients to pay fines via a QR code.
Residents are advised to delete suspicious messages and verify any court notices through official channels.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, goes to trial for assault with a dangerous weapon on March 23.
The trial is a bench trial overseen by Judge Jeffrey Karp in Essex Superior Court, with Fitzsimmons waiving her right to a jury.
Fitzsimmons was shot by a fellow officer during an incident involving a restraining order at her home last summer.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos, 44, from Cali, Colombia, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
She runs to support Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital, inspired by patients rebuilding their lives step by step.
Sinisterra Mazariegos views running as a metaphor for life, emphasizing patience and small, consistent efforts.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues with The Chats on March 20, 2026.
The Hives displayed consistent enthusiasm and seasoned performance, captivating the audience with new and classic hits.
Opening act The Chats delivered an energetic performance with a distinct garage punk style.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, a 42-year-old from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023, McHugh overcame treatment challenges to return to marathon running.
McHugh runs in memory of friends and loved ones, including her mentor Susan Hurley who passed away recently.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and others question Meta's plans to integrate facial recognition in smart glasses, citing privacy risks.
Senators argue the technology could identify protesters and chill free expression, demanding Meta disclose data protection measures.
Meta has until April 6 to respond to concerns, as protests are planned across Massachusetts on March 28.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council delayed voting on a $2.65 million bond for new firefighter gear free of PFAS.
Current firefighting gear in Quincy tested positive for PFAS, chemicals linked to cancer and health risks.
City officials seek two new sets of turnout gear per firefighter to reduce long-term PFAS exposure.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposed transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address $57 million in needed repairs.
The zoo, facing declining attendance and high expenses, would focus on native wildlife and rehabilitation under the new plan.
Zoo officials express concern over the proposal, while a transition team is expected to form in July to guide changes.
